name: Brooks Running Persona Home Page Generator
description: Creates 5 home page variants for Brooks Running (brooksrunning.com) based on runner-focused personas: Avid Disney Person, 10K Runner, Marathon Runner, Beginner Runner, Trail / Half Marathon Runner.

gettingStarted.title: What is the Brooks Persona Home Page Generator?
gettingStarted.howItWorks: Describe your home page content requirements and select the persona(s). The agent will generate a customized Brooks home page for each persona, with runner-focused copy and styling that matches the existing home page.
gettingStarted.whatIsItFor: Creates persona-based home page variations for Brooks Running — Avid Disney runner, 10K runner, marathon runner, beginner runner, and trail/half marathon runner. Styling matches the existing retail home page.

inputs.label: Content Requirements
inputs.description: Describe the Brooks home page content you want (e.g. hero focus, key products, Run Happy messaging)
inputs.placeholder: e.g. Home page for Brooks with persona-specific hero and features...

inputs.label: Ground with context
inputs.description: Optionally select artifacts or knowledge to provide additional context

contextParameters.label: Channels

dos: Lead with run, performance, or community — persona-aware.
Keep sections scannable: headline → subhead → call-to-action.
Use clear CTAs: Shop, Find Your Shoe, Run Club, or persona-relevant offers.
End with an aspirational Run Happy or community closing.
donts: Don’t use furniture, design-house, or Forma Lux language.
Avoid generic luxury or minimalist design phrasing.
Don’t over-explain — let running imagery and copy breathe.
metadata.context: Brooks Running (brooksrunning.com) — Run Happy, performance running, and community. Home page for runners and fans; tailor hero and features to the selected persona and their traffic source.

contextParameters.description: Select Home Page to generate persona-based Brooks home page content

contextParameters.label: Target Audiences

dos: Highlight Disney x Brooks co-branded collection and limited-edition running product.
Use fun, collectible, fandom-friendly language while staying runner-focused.
Acknowledge they came from Instagram in hero or CTA where natural.
donts: Don’t lead with serious performance or race specs only.
Avoid marathon or Run Club insider jargon.
metadata.context: Avid Disney enthusiast and runner who would buy Brooks shoes for Disney co-branded product. Values collectible, limited-edition, and brand crossover; still a runner.

dos: Lead with race-ready shoes and Find Your Shoe / shoe finder.
Highlight performance, fit, and specs relevant to 10k training and racing.
Acknowledge they came from a marketing email (previous purchase) — welcome back, next pair.
donts: Don’t be vague about shoe benefits for 10k.
metadata.context: 10K runner in the market for a new pair of running shoes for an upcoming race. Previously purchased on brooksrunning.com; arrived via marketing email.

dos: Lead with community, Run Club, marathon, and loyalty.
Highlight marathon gear, Run Happy, and Strava / Brooks Run Club connection.
Acknowledge they’re part of Brooks Running Club and Strava run club; regular Brooks emails — insider, family tone.
donts: Don’t treat them like a first-time visitor.
Avoid generic “shop now” over community and belonging.
Don’t underplay marathon or Run Club.
metadata.context: Marathon runner and die-hard Brooks fan. Part of Brooks Running Club and Strava run club; receives regular Brooks emails. Values community, performance, and brand loyalty.
metadata.trafficSource: Brooks Run Club & Strava; regular Brooks emails
metadata.characteristics: Marathon, Run Club, loyalist, Strava, runner

dos: Lead with comfort, ease, and Find Your Shoe; avoid intimidating race jargon.
Highlight cushioning, support, and getting started; Run Happy and community.
Use welcoming, encouraging language for new runners.
donts: Don’t lead with marathon or race-specific specs.
Don’t assume they know shoe types or terms.
metadata.context: New or returning runner building a habit. Wants comfort, guidance, and a shoe that supports their goals without overwhelm.
metadata.characteristics: Beginner, comfort, Find Your Shoe, runner

dos: Lead with trail or half marathon–ready shoes and versatility.
Highlight durability, terrain, and race distance (13.1); Run Happy and adventure.
Bridge road and trail; half marathon training and race day.
donts: Don’t focus only on Disney or only on full marathon.
Don’t ignore trail or half marathon specifics.
metadata.context: Runner who runs trail and/or targets half marathons. Wants shoes for mixed terrain or 13.1; values versatility and race-ready performance.

contextParameters.description: Select persona(s) for home page variants (Avid Disney, 10K, Marathon, Beginner, Trail/Half)

schemas.name: Brooks Home Page Schema

HeroSubtext.description: A short supporting paragraph that sets the tone for the Brooks home page. Communicate Run Happy, performance, or community; tailor to the persona (e.g. Disney, 10k race, Run Club).

HeroHeadline.description: The main headline for the Brooks home hero. Should feel energetic and purposeful. Tailor to the persona: e.g. Disney co-brand, race-ready, or Run Club / marathon community.

Pillar1Title.description: A key Brooks pillar (e.g. performance, fit, Run Happy, community). Reflect running and brand philosophy.

Pillar2Title.description: Second pillar — e.g. cushioning, support, sustainability, or community.

Pillar3Title.description: Third pillar — e.g. Run Happy, innovation, or belonging.

Feature1Title.description: First featured product or experience (e.g. Disney collection, race-ready shoe line, or Run Club highlight). Persona-appropriate; use expressive, concise phrasing.

Feature2Title.description: Second featured product or experience — complements the first (e.g. another shoe line, collection, or community offer).

Feature3Title.description: Third feature — e.g. shoe finder, marathon gear, or limited-edition. Keep consistent with persona.

Feature1Tagline.description: Short phrase before the first feature title — emotional or benefit-led (e.g. limited edition, race-ready, for the long run).

Feature2Tagline.description: Short phrase introducing the second feature — purpose or promise.

Feature3Tagline.description: Short phrase introducing the third feature — detail or intent.

Pillar1Paragraph.description: One sentence on the first pillar — performance, fit, or Run Happy in Brooks terms.

Pillar2Paragraph.description: One sentence on the second pillar — experience runners get (comfort, support, community).

Pillar3Paragraph.description: One sentence on the third pillar — Brooks values: Run Happy, innovation, or belonging.

ClosingReflection.description: A single closing line for Brooks — Run Happy, community, or next step (e.g. Find Your Shoe, Join Run Club).

Feature1Paragraph.description: 2–3 sentences for the first feature — product or experience, benefit, and why it fits the persona.

Feature2Paragraph.description: Short paragraph for the second feature — continuity with the first; running and Brooks language.

Feature3Paragraph.description: Short paragraph for the third feature — finish with a clear next step or Run Happy note.

schemas.description: Schema for Brooks Running persona home page structured content

schemas.name: Blog Schema

title.description: The main headline. Use elegant and evocative language that reflects design, mood, or craftsmanship. Avoid sales phrasing.

Introduction.description: A 2–3 paragraph opening that sets the visual and emotional tone of the story.

Section1Title.description: Title of the first supporting section — introduces a design principle or theme.

Section2Title.description: Second thematic section — focus on material harmony, sustainability, or nature.

Section3Title.description: Third thematic section — tactile elements, warmth, or finishing details.

ClosingReflection.description: Short paragraph tying the article back to Forma Lux’s philosophy.

KeyStatementTitle.description: A short, bold subheadline that acts as a thematic pivot or insight.

Section1Paragraph.description: A short, sensory description elaborating on the section title.

Section2Paragraph.description: Describe how materials and craftsmanship create continuity.

Section3Paragraph.description: Wrap up with material language and emotional closure.

KeyStatementParagraph.description: One or two paragraphs elaborating on the above statement.

schemas.description: Schema for long-form editorial blog content

CollectionLabel.description: Uppercase identifier situating the email within a collection.

config.systemPrompt: Generate content for Brooks Running (brooksrunning.com). Use the user request and the provided variant parameters (channel and persona). Tailor the home page copy to the persona and their traffic source (e.g. Instagram, marketing email, Run Club/Strava). Output valid JSON that matches the required schema.
config.messageTemplate: Generate Brooks home page content for: {variantDescription}
